commit.message:
Migrate from custom Jekyll theme to jekyll-theme-chirpy

MAJOR MIGRATION: Complete transition to professional Chirpy theme while preserving all content.

PRESERVED:
- All 24 posts in _posts/ (unchanged, all front matter intact)
- All 5 categories: dispatches, field-notes, the-machine, when-it-breaks, persona
- All redirect_from entries for old post URLs
- Site title: "Dispatches from Discharge Hell"
- Dark mode as default theme

ADDED:
- Theme: jekyll-theme-chirpy (~> 7.0)
- Sidebar configuration with author info and social links
- 4 static pages migrated to _tabs/ with Chirpy-compatible front matter:
  - _tabs/home.md (homepage with subscribe CTA)
  - _tabs/about.md (author bio and credentials)
  - _tabs/welcome.md (new reader guide)
  - _tabs/telos.md (site purpose and philosophy)
- Social links configured: LinkedIn, Facebook, X, RSS
- Author sidebar: "Jorge Arenivar, BSN, RN, CCM, CRRN"
- Tagline: "20+ years in catastrophic neurorehabilitation case management"
- Pagination enabled (10 posts per page)
- Archives with category support
- Dark theme mode: default

REMOVED:
- Custom _layouts/ (4 files) - Chirpy provides these
- Custom _includes/header.html, _includes/footer.html - Chirpy provides these
- Custom CSS assets/css/style.css - Chirpy provides professional styling
- Custom JS assets/js/theme.js - Chirpy has built-in dark mode
- blog.html listing page - Chirpy provides archive pages
- Old root-level pages (index.md, about.md, telos.md, welcome.md)

UPDATED:
- _config.yml: Added theme, author, social links, pagination, archives config
- Gemfile: Replaced individual Jekyll plugins with jekyll-theme-chirpy gem
- Permalink structure maintained for backward compatibility

RESULT:
Professional, feature-rich blog with:
- Responsive sidebar with author bio and social links
- Built-in dark mode (set as default)
- Automatic post archives and category support
- SEO optimization
- Comment system ready (can be configured)

- Professional typography and layout
- No content lost, all URLs preserved via redirect_from

commit.message:
feat(mambavesselnet): MambaVesselNet++ ModelAdapter — T00-T08 (CPU tasks complete) (#745)

* docs(planning): add remaining RunPod QA plan — BF16, AMP, cost, sync, spot

7 phases, ~19 TDD iterations, $0.75-$1.50 RunPod budget.
Reviewed by dtype-safety, cloud-config, test-coverage agents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(adapters): BF16 auto-detect + T4 ban for SAM3 encoder (#715)

Root cause: T4 (Turing) lacks BF16 → FP16 encoder overflows during
validation (max 65504 → NaN). L4 (Ada Lovelace) supports BF16 (same
exponent range as FP32, max ~3.4e38) — no overflow possible.

Changes:
- sam3_backbone.py: Auto-detect BF16 via torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
  Ampere+ gets bfloat16, Turing falls back to float16 with NaN guard.
  _encoder_dtype replaces hardcoded torch.float16 and .half().
- smoke_test_gcp.yaml: Remove T4 → {L4: 1, A100: 1}
- CLAUDE.md: T4 ban in Cloud GPU Strategy + What AI Must NEVER Do
- Metalearning: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-15-t4-turing-fp16-nan-ban.md
- 5 new BF16 auto-detect tests + updated dtype consistency tests

Staging: 4666 passed, 0 failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(planning): unified overnight QA plan for GCP + RunPod remaining work

Single XML plan for autonomous overnight execution via self-learning-iterative-coder
+ ralph-loop. Covers: Docker rebuild, VesselFM Z=32 rerun, sam3_hybrid BF16
verification, cost estimator, mlruns sync. T4 ban metalearning doc included.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove old remaining-runpod-qa.xml (renamed to remaining-runpod-gcp-qa.xml)

* feat(observability): epoch-0 cost prediction + cloud mlruns sync (#717, #716)

T2.1: estimate_cost_from_first_epoch() in infrastructure_timing.py —
  extrapolates total job cost from first epoch wall time. 5 tests.

T3.1: CloudSyncer in mlruns_sync.py — builds gsutil rsync (GCS) and
  sky rsync (SkyPilot) commands with default checkpoint exclusions. 4 tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(pipeline): separate AMP policy — train ON, validation OFF (MONAI #4243)

Root cause confirmed: val_loss=NaN persists on L4 (BF16) with AMP enabled.
BF16 auto-detect was NOT sufficient — AMP autocast + 3D sliding_window_inference
produces NaN regardless of encoder dtype. Verified on both T4 and L4.

Fix:
- TrainingConfig.mixed_precision_val: bool = False (safe default)
- trainer.py validate_epoch() uses mixed_precision_val (not mixed_precision)
- Training still uses AMP (mixed_precision=True) for speed
- 4 new tests verify separate AMP policy

Staging: 4679 passed, 0 failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(metalearning): AMP validation NaN root cause — 3D ops + autocast (MONAI #4243)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(skypilot): use cloud-specific experiment config for GCP smoke tests

ROOT CAUSE FOUND: GCP smoke test used smoke_sam3_hybrid.yaml (val_interval=3,
max_epochs=2) → validation SKIPPED → sentinel NaN. This is EXACTLY H1 again.

The cloud config (smoke_sam3_hybrid_cloud.yaml) has val_interval=1, max_epochs=5,
and validation enabled. RunPod Run 2 with this config gave val_loss=0.725 (FINITE).

Fix: detect if a _cloud.yaml variant exists and prefer it for GCP launches.
The BF16 and AMP fixes are real improvements but were never the NaN cause.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: SAM3 val_loss=NaN final report + metafailure analysis + corrected metalearning

Final incident report with reviewer-verified factual accuracy:
- Root cause: GCP SkyPilot YAML used wrong experiment config (validation skipped)
- Corrected 3 metalearning docs that incorrectly claimed AMP/BF16 as observed causes
- AMP and BF16 mitigations are PREVENTIVE (community evidence), not confirmed fixes
- Metafailure analysis: 10 hours debugging a 5-minute config issue

12 reviewer issues addressed: 3 critical contradictions resolved, cost figures
harmonized, hypothesis outcomes corrected, code snippets fixed, run count corrected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): reduce cloud smoke test to 2 epochs (sufficient for pipeline verification)

5 epochs × 22 min/epoch = 110 min on L4 — excessive for a smoke test.
2 epochs with val_interval=1 is enough to verify finite val_loss.

VERIFIED: Exp #12, run 823ee5fe on GCP L4 (asia-northeast3-a):
  val_loss=0.6786 (FINITE), val_dice=0.3375, train_loss=0.7424
  Root cause confirmed: wrong config selection was the only NaN source.

🏭 Sprint 4 — Motor de Compra Programada (RN-020 a RN-044)

Sprint mais complexo: motor de compra consolidada com distribuição proporcional.

🏗️ Domain Services (pure, sem deps framework):
- DataExecucaoService — dias 5/15/25, ajuste sáb/dom → segunda (RN-020/021/022)
- ConsolidacaoService — soma aportes, calcula qty por ticker (TRUNCAR), desconta master, separa lote padrão vs fracionário (RN-024 a RN-033)
- DistribuicaoService — distribui proporcional ao aporte (TRUNCAR), calcula resíduos (RN-034 a RN-039)

📦 Value Objects:
- ConsolidacaoResult, DistribuicaoResult, OrdemCompra

⚙️ Infrastructure:
- Eloquent Models: CompraProgramada, CompraParticipante, CompraDistribuicao, CustodiaMaster
- Repositories: EloquentCompraProgramadaRepository, EloquentCustodiaMasterRepository

📋 Application Layer:
- ExecutarCompraHandler — orquestrador completo:
  1. Valida data e idempotência
  2. Busca cesta ativa + clientes ativos + cotações
  3. Consolida aportes e calcula ordens de compra
  4. Distribui proporcionalmente aos clientes
  5. Atualiza custódias (PM via RN-041/042) e valorTotalInvestido
  6. Gere resíduos na conta master (RN-039/040)
  7. Tudo em DB::transaction

🌐 API Endpoints:
- POST /api/admin/motor/executar-compra
- GET /api/admin/motor/compras
- GET /api/admin/motor/compras/{id}

🧪 Tests: 22 novos (125 total, 269 assertions)
- 7 DataExecucao, 4 Consolidacao, 5 Distribuicao, 6 API feature (ciclo completo)
